Zone: CLI Interface Layer (`packages-rex:cli-interface-layer`)
Files: 38, Cohesion: 0.51, Coupling: 0.49

Description: Implements the interactive and programmatic CLI surface of Rex including chunked review workflows, MCP server bootstrapping, output formatting, project configuration access, and miscellaneous management commands.

</imports>

<insights>

- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- The bidirectional flow between this zone and unit-analyze (56 inbound, 32 outbound) confirms the two zones have grown entangled; commands like analyze.ts and smart-add.ts that live in unit-analyze likely belong here conceptually.
- src/cli/mcp.ts as an entry point is architecturally significant — the MCP server is the primary integration surface for Claude Code tooling and should be treated as a stable public API boundary.
- chunked-review.ts and chunked-review-state.ts represent a stateful multi-turn interaction pattern that is distinct from other stateless commands; isolating this into its own sub-module would clarify the state management surface.
- The bidirectional dependency with unit-analyze (cli → unit-analyze: 56, unit-analyze → cli: 32) and with core (cli → core: 38, core → cli: 15) suggests the CLI layer boundary is not enforced — business logic has leaked both in and out.
- store/project-config.ts appearing as an entry point in this zone rather than in the store zone indicates a cross-zone boundary violation; this file should live in and be exported from the store zone.
- cli/mcp.ts as an isolated entry point for MCP server bootstrapping is a clean separation of transport concerns from command logic.
- Zone "cli-interface-layer" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The three-zone triangle of cli-interface-layer ↔ prd-analysis-engine ↔ prd-domain-operations (core), with all three pairs bidirectionally coupled and call volumes exceeding 50 in every direction, forms a dependency knot: no single zone can be extracted, mocked, or replaced without modifying the other two simultaneously.
- The 65 call-graph edges from cli-interface-layer → prd-domain-operations combined with 15 reverse imports from core → cli means CLI command handlers are not just consuming domain operations — they are being called back by domain logic, suggesting domain state transitions are dispatching through CLI output utilities to report progress.
- The three-way bidirectional coupling between cli-interface-layer, prd-analysis-engine, and prd-domain-operations (core) constitutes a dependency knot: all three zones import from each other with call volumes above 50 per pair. This prevents independent testing, extraction, or replacement of any single zone.
- core → cli (15 imports, part of the 65 call-graph edges flowing back from domain into CLI) suggests domain operations are calling CLI output utilities for progress reporting. Side-effect I/O should be injected into domain logic, not imported from the CLI layer.
- src/analyze/reshape-reason.ts is classified in cli-interface-layer despite residing in src/analyze/ — its name describes a domain transformation (reshaping analysis output), not a presentation concern; this is a misplaced file that contributes to the bidirectional coupling with prd-analysis-engine.
- The CLI layer is the only mechanism for progress feedback from the analysis engine (214 inbound call-graph edges). This makes it structurally impossible to invoke the analysis engine from the MCP server, programmatic API, or any non-CLI context without importing CLI output utilities — a hidden runtime constraint with no indication at the type level.
- src/analyze/reshape-reason.ts is misplaced: its path and purpose (transforming analysis data shapes) indicate it is domain logic belonging in prd-analysis-engine, not a CLI presentation concern. Its current placement is a primary source of the bidirectional cli ↔ prd-analysis-engine coupling.
- The CLI output layer is the sole progress-reporting mechanism for the analysis engine (214 call-graph edges). Invoking prd-analysis-engine from the MCP HTTP server or any programmatic context silently imports and executes CLI output code. An EventEmitter or callback injection at the analyze/index.ts boundary would decouple the two zones entirely.
- store → cli: 5 combined with cli → store: 20 means the persistence layer makes 5 callback imports into the CLI layer. A persistence store importing CLI utilities implies it contains CLI-aware error messages or output formatting — coupling storage semantics to terminal presentation. This is a secondary instance of the same domain→CLI inversion present in prd-analysis-engine, indicating the pattern is systemic rather than isolated.
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts is imported by 6 zones and lives inside cli-interface-layer — every non-CLI zone that needs shared constants must take a transitive compile-time dependency on the CLI package. This means removing or renaming a CLI command forces a rebuild of every zone that uses a shared constant, even if the constant itself is unchanged. The blast radius of CLI changes is artificially amplified by this placement.
- The chunked-review-state.ts file in cli-interface-layer introduces stateful session management into the CLI layer, making it the only stateful component in a layer that is otherwise a thin dispatch wrapper. This statefulness is architecturally anomalous for a CLI command module and will prevent the CLI layer from being safely parallelized or reused in non-interactive contexts (e.g., MCP batch operations).
- store → cli: 5 imports indicate the persistence layer contains CLI-aware logic (likely output formatting or error messages). This is the same domain→CLI inversion found in prd-analysis-engine, confirming it is a package-wide pattern rather than an isolated case. A single shared output-primitives module extracted from cli-interface-layer would eliminate both inversion sites without restructuring either zone.
- chunked-review-state.ts introduces stateful multi-turn session management directly into the CLI command layer. Session state held in CLI command modules cannot be accessed by the MCP server (src/cli/mcp.ts) or any programmatic caller — if chunked review is ever needed in non-interactive contexts, the state management layer must be duplicated. Extract session state into the store zone to make it transport-agnostic.
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts has a 6-zone import fan-in but is classified inside cli-interface-layer. Its presence there forces every consumer zone to take a compile-time dependency on the CLI package boundary even when only needing a constant value. Moving it to a standalone shared-constants module would reduce cli-interface-layer's artificial import fan-out and decouple constants from CLI volatility.
- [call graph] 1382 internal calls, 207 outgoing, 305 incoming (cohesion: 0.87, coupling: 0.13)

</insights>
